I'm happy to answer it. I didn't want to step on your toes. So this is my chance to get to say we'll talk more about this later. But the big idea is that when you are doing any kind of marketing, there are different ways you can assign credit. You can assign credit to maybe the first interaction, right? So the time someone is introduced to your business and we would call that first touch attribution. So if somebody sees a Facebook ad and six months later buys through email, you might give the credit to Facebook. Or you can do something called last touch attribution. And that instead looks at the last interaction someone had before they purchased. So in the example I just gave, email rather than Facebook would get credit for the sale. So there's no one way to attribute credit. It's kind of the most important thing to understand. It really depends on how you want to measure. When you are a newer business, you're trying to get your feet under you. You don't have cash reserves. You're still trying to prove your offer. You really want to stick to last touch attribution.
